topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the email, his fingers hovering over the 'send' button as if it were a live wire. He reread the job description one last time, a faint tremor running through his hands. It was the Senior Analyst position at OmniCorp, a role he’d been meticulously preparing for. Then he saw the subject line: "Application Received." A slow, almost stunned exhale escaped him. He blinked several times, forcing his gaze back to the screen. His jaw slackened slightly. He leaned back in his chair, the cheap plastic groaning under his weight, and let out a soft, disbelieving laugh that caught in his throat. It was as if the floor had tilted, revealing a vista he hadn't anticipated.

[story 2]

"No way," Maya whispered, her voice barely audible over the hum of the coffee machine. She watched Liam’s face as he scrolled through his inbox, his expression shifting from focused concentration to utter bewilderment. When his eyes finally landed on the confirmation notification, he physically recoiled, clutching his mug so tightly his knuckles turned white. He slowly lowered the mug, his eyes wide, tracking the text as if it were a foreign language. A small, involuntary sound—a sharp intake of breath—escaped him. He shook his head once, a jerky movement, as if trying to dislodge a sudden, impossible thought. The sheer improbability of the situation seemed to hang heavy in the air between them.

[story 3]

I reread the notification three times, my heart hammering against my ribs with an erratic rhythm. It was the same opening to the OmniCorp application portal. My initial reaction was pure disbelief; I actually had to physically stop myself from grinning so broadly that it hurt my cheeks. I leaned forward, resting my elbows on the desk, my gaze fixed on the screen, a slow, appreciative smile spreading across my face. The world seemed to have momentarily paused, offering this unexpected, brilliant twist to my carefully laid plans. I let out a low whistle, a sound of pure, unadulterated wonder.
